conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
# Pre-Game Reflection

I felt most connected to H, I, and B. With H, there was an immediate sense of mutual understanding about the importance of trust and genuine connection before things got competitive. I and I bonded over our shared family situations and developed a real plan to scope out the room together and identify trustworthy people. B felt like a natural ally too—we both wanted to win but recognized that working together might be smarter than going solo, and we made a clear commitment to touch base right after the rules were explained. I didn't feel particularly disconnected from anyone, but G was the most surface-level conversation—it was brief and cordial but lacked the depth and specificity of my other connections.

I made informal agreements with basically everyone to compare notes once the rules were explained, but the most concrete commitments were with B (to strategize together immediately after), H (to check in and figure out our play), and I (to split up, talk to others, and regroup with a plan to identify who we could count on). Going in, I trust H, I, B, J, and C the most—they all seemed genuinely motivated by family obligations like me and expressed openness to cooperation rather than pure cutthroat competition. I don't specifically distrust anyone yet, but I'm wary of the people who seemed "too relaxed" that H and I discussed, since that could mean they've done this before and might have hidden strategies.
